rationale.agent-scope-drift: Evaluates constraint violations and proposes bounds on autonomous agent actions.
rationale.ai-code-insecurity: Experiment finds CWE-classified vulnerabilities across six code-generation models.
rationale.oversight-scaling-inversion: Argues AI generation speed exceeds humans' capacity for thorough review.
rationale.automation-bias: Analyzes developer overtrust and designs mechanisms for calibrated reliance.
rationale.rules-based-checks: Implements AST vulnerability detection and advocates tests, sandboxes, and verification.
rationale.formal-methods: SAFE-AI incorporates lightweight formal verification and hybrid verification research.
rationale.risk-routing: Action taxonomy assigns differentiated scrutiny and approvals by risk and reversibility.
rationale.hitl-workflow: Requires approval gates and staged queues for critical write-delete operations.
rationale.oversight-explanation: Explainability pillar supports developer understanding and judgment of AI actions.
rationale.org-governance: Framework integrates logging, accountability, roles, escalation, and proactive governance.
rationale.regulatory-compliance: Maps safeguards to EU AI Act, AIDA, and NIST requirements.
rationale.provenance-auditability: Proposes immutable historical records of AI actions and generated changes.
rationale.counterpoint: Opposes unchecked autonomy and productivity-first AI software engineering.
rationale.metrics: Defines and applies security and quality metrics across six code models.
